Same outward behavior as s06 from the user's perspective. The diff is what
the SkillLoader prints in verbose mode and what it would feed into s08's
ContextBuilder once that lands.

$ go run . -workspace /tmp/agent-demo -session demo -v "list .go files"
[s07] loaded 2 skill(s) from workspace="/tmp/agent-demo/skills" builtin=".../testdata/skills"
       - echo (ok) — Echo a string back as-is
       - weather (ok) — Get current weather for a city
bash_tool.go bus.go main.go memory.go provider.go provider_anthropic.go provider_fake.go registry.go runner.go session.go skills.go tools.go

$ ls /tmp/agent-demo
.cursor
history.jsonl

The skills directory is loaded by SkillLoader; the memory files are written
by MemoryStore. Two completely independent concerns sharing one workspace.

If `curl` is uninstalled (rare on macOS/Linux), the weather skill flips to
unavailable:
       - weather (unavailable: CLI: curl) — Get current weather for a city

If you author a workspace skill with the same name as a builtin, the
workspace version wins:

$ mkdir -p /tmp/agent-demo/skills/weather
$ cat > /tmp/agent-demo/skills/weather/SKILL.md <<'EOF'
---
name: weather
description: my custom weather skill (paid API)
requires:
  bins: [curl]
---
Use my paid endpoint at https://my-weather.example.com.
EOF

$ go run . -workspace /tmp/agent-demo -v "test"
[s07] loaded 2 skill(s) ...
       - echo (ok) — Echo a string back as-is
       - weather (ok) — my custom weather skill (paid API)
                                ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
                                workspace override visible

If you mark a skill `enabled: false`, it disappears from List() entirely.
If you pass -disabled-skills weather, it disappears the same way (different
mechanism, same effect — config vs. content).

If you write a malformed SKILL.md (bad YAML), it's silently skipped:
the loader's invariant is "one bad skill never breaks the whole pipeline".
The error is dropped, not propagated — log/warn pathway is left to s08.

Concurrent behavior:

$ go test -count=1 -race ./...
ok      learn-nanobot/s07       1.4s

The 7 tests cover frontmatter parse, malformed-skip, missing-bin filter,
LoadForContext concatenation, disabled list, workspace-overrides-builtin,
and enabled:false opt-out. Run with -race; no goroutines start in the
loader (it's pure file I/O), so the race detector mostly verifies that
the surrounding scaffolding (Bus/SessionManager/MemoryStore) still passes
its s06 invariants.
